specification.topic-statement: The provider serialization subsystem translates a canonical LLM request structure into the HTTP request body format required by each supported provider (Anthropic, OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses, and Google Gemini), and translates each provider's streaming SSE events back into a normalized stream event sequence.

scope.in-scope: The canonical request structure, request body serialization differences for each provider, tool schema serialization, thinking configuration serialization, streaming event normalization, and the unified stream event types emitted to the REPL layer.
scope.boundary: How the serialized request body is transmitted over HTTP and how raw bytes are delivered to the streaming callback is covered by the HTTP multiplexer specification.
How raw HTTP response bytes are split into SSE event frames is covered by the SSE parser specification.
How normalized stream events are processed by the REPL to update the display and transition agent state is covered by the LLM response handling specification.

field.name: system prompt
field.note: the assembled system prompt; null if none

field.name: messages
field.note: conversation turns from the context window start index

field.name: thinking
field.note: level and budget; determines whether thinking parameters are included

field.name: max output tokens
field.note: upper bound on response length; absent when not explicitly set

field.name: tools
field.note: name, description, and JSON schema for each tool

field.name: tool choice
field.note: one of: auto, none, any, or a specific tool name

field.name: type
field.note: one of: start, text_delta, thinking_delta, tool_call_start, tool_call_delta, tool_call_done, done, error

field.name: text
field.note: present for text_delta and thinking_delta events

field.name: finish reason
field.note: present for done; indicates whether the response ended naturally, with a tool call, or due to a content filter

behavior.anthropic-request-serialization:
The Anthropic Messages API format maps the canonical request as follows:
- The system prompt maps to the top-level `system` field. - Each message maps to the `messages` array with its role and content blocks. - Text blocks serialize as objects with `type: "text"`. - Tool call blocks serialize as `type: "tool_use"` with `id`, `name`, and `input` (the arguments parsed from JSON string to object). - Tool result blocks serialize as `type: "tool_result"` with `tool_use_id`, `content`, and `is_error`. - Thinking blocks serialize as `type: "thinking"` with `text` and `signature`. - Redacted thinking blocks serialize as `type: "redacted_thinking"` with `data`. - Tool schemas use `input_schema` (not `parameters`). - Thinking configuration maps to two paths based on model type: - Budget-based models: a `thinking` object with `type: "enabled"` and a numeric `budget_tokens` value derived from the thinking level. - Adaptive models: an `output_config` object with a string effort level (`"low"`, `"medium"`, `"high"`).
behavior.openai-chat-completions-serialization:
The OpenAI Chat Completions API format differs from Anthropic in several ways:
- The system prompt is prepended as a message with role `"system"`. - Tool call arguments remain as a JSON string in `function.arguments` (not parsed to an object). - Tool result messages use role `"tool"` with `tool_call_id` and `content`. - Tool schemas use `"parameters"` (not `"input_schema"`). - Thinking maps to `"reasoning_effort"` with string values (`"low"`, `"medium"`, `"high"`). - The maximum token field is named `"max_completion_tokens"`. - Streaming requests include a `stream_options` object with `include_usage: true` to receive token counts in the final chunk.
behavior.openai-responses-api-serialization:
The OpenAI Responses API (for newer reasoning models) uses a distinct format:
- The system prompt becomes a top-level `"instructions"` field rather than a message. - Single-turn single-text requests may use a string `input` field; multi-turn conversations use an array format. - The maximum token field is named `"max_output_tokens"`. - Reasoning effort is included only for reasoning-capable models.
behavior.google-gemini-serialization:
The Google Gemini API format uses a substantially different structure:
- The system prompt maps to `systemInstruction.parts[]` as a text part. - Messages map to `contents[]` with roles `"user"`, `"model"`, or `"function"`. - Text parts use the `text` field. - Thinking parts include `"thought": true` alongside the text. - Tool calls serialize as `functionCall` parts with `name` and `args` (the arguments as a parsed object, not a string). - Tool results serialize as `functionResponse` parts with `name` and a nested `response` object. - Tools are wrapped as `functionDeclarations` inside a `tools` array. - Tool choice modes map to Gemini's `mode` string values: `"NONE"`, `"AUTO"`, `"ANY"`. - Thinking configuration maps to `thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget` (numeric, for Gemini 2.5) or `thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel` (string, for newer models).
behavior.stream-event-normalization:
Each provider emits SSE events in its own format. Each provider's streaming handler translates these into the normalized stream event types:
**Anthropic**: `content_block_start` identifies the block type; subsequent `content_block_delta` events carry `text_delta`, `input_json_delta` (for tool arguments), or `thinking_delta`. Usage arrives in `message_delta`. Finish reason arrives in `message_delta.stop_reason`.
**OpenAI Chat Completions**: Each SSE data line is a JSON chunk under `choices[0].delta`. Text arrives in `content`; tool arguments arrive in `tool_calls[].function.arguments`. A `[DONE]` sentinel marks the end. Usage arrives in the final chunk when `include_usage` was requested.
**OpenAI Responses API**: Named events (`response.output_text.delta`, `response.function_call_arguments.delta`, `response.reasoning_summary_text.delta`, `response.completed`) carry content and metadata.
**Google Gemini**: Each SSE data line is a complete JSON chunk. `content.parts` contains text parts, thinking parts (marked with `"thought": true`), and `functionCall` parts. Usage arrives in `usageMetadata` of the final chunk.
All four providers normalize their events to the same set of typed stream events before delivering them to the REPL response handler.
behavior.tool-schema-serialization: Tool definitions are included in every request. For each tool, the name, description, and complete JSON schema for its parameters are included. Anthropic receives the schema under the key `input_schema`; all other providers receive it under `parameters`. The schema object itself is the same in all cases — only the wrapping key differs.
